title: How do I stop "(copy)" from being included in my Item duplicates?
excerpt: We have to create several copies of Items in each of our Groups. Currently, we duplicate each Item (row) several times. Unfortunately, every time I duplicate it adds, “(copy)” in the name field of each Project in the gro…

posts.author: Hollywood_Jeff (Jeff Peri)
posts.cookedHtml: We have to create several copies of Items in each of our Groups. Currently, we duplicate each Item (row) several times. Unfortunately, every time I duplicate it adds, “(copy)” in the name field of each Project in the group. So, I have to go through each Project Item and delete the word “copy”. How do I stop “(copy)” from being included in my Item duplicates? Is there an option to stop the automated addition of “copy” to these fields when I duplicate?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is no option to stop this unfortunately.

You can use the app Column Magic to remove them with an automation for when items are created, it can remove copy. At least its not manual!

Update Magic is a second option, which can do the duplicate and remove in a single action. This may be preferable, since it will consume less automations - if your duplication is by automation. But if its a manual duplication (or thats a regular part of your workflow) I’d use the Column Magic one.

posts.author: Hollywood_Jeff (Jeff Peri)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the info! Not really looking to pay for another app. I will request it as a feature.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I would expect it to be implement as a native feature sometime in 2038

posts.author: Hollywood_Jeff (Jeff Peri)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you provide me with an automation?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I can provide it through the aforementioned Column Magic I do not know if or when monday.com will make it a native feature.

posts.author: Anthony11989 (Antonio Armendariz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Oh wow this is awesome! I’m looking into building applications and well the FAQ’s are a little confusing. Would you be able to provide detail on how you created this? Maybe we connect direct or whatever is best for you.

I created something like this but more of a webhook and its automated upon Duplicate automation inputs though I don’t know how to make this an App. Here is my github link, describing how I set it up.

Anthony11989/Copy-Remove-Suffix: Remove (Copy) Suffix from duplicated items in Monday.com

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks, boss.
I would suggest looking into these welcome apps by monday:

github.com

GitHub - mondaycom/welcome-apps: A collection of "Hello World" monday.com apps

A collection of "Hello World" monday.com apps

On the question of how I made it work:
when you use the Monday SDK, it provides you with context. So if your app is in different “real estates,” you get different context, like an item view or board view.

Once a user opens my app and selects items, in the client side (using the Monday SDK) fetches those items. Then I call a simple JS function to remove all the copies from the item. When you click “Remove Copy,” I call a mutation using the Monday SDK to update it.

I hope that helps.
I would recommend these docs:

image1920×1049 105 KB

Also, use Kapa AI. It’s really good. Just ask it how to do something or how to write queries, and it will do it for you.

One of the best uses of AI by the Monday team.

Hey!

We have a small internal app - Remove (copy) suffix.

It is quite simple, there is just one automation you add to the board:

image3456×1978 405 KB

And done. It’s that simple.

App is in the process of review so it will be published in the marketplace. It will remain fully free.

So yeah, hope I helped!

posts.author: Anthony11989 (Antonio Armendariz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ayye! It’s me again… Circling back letting you know that I figured it out. I built an app using multi-item menu board. The app triggers when you select multiple duplicate item names that may have different data you want to pick and choose to combine once you’ve selected what you want and then create a new item with the same name and only the data you want.

Thanks again for the hints and mentioning about kapa ai

posts.author: Anthony11989 (Antonio Armendariz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ohh wow! Such great timing! I was actually going to post about file handling assistance; however, I think you provided an amazing idea! In long terms this is what I’m understanding from your response. Since monday.com has exemptions on file handling through javascript/graphql when it comes to downloading on their public server and reuploading into a new item (still trying to figure that out… I can just “over-write” the item name that contain the needed files and manipulate the rest of the columns to have new data output from another item’s data I need. Let me know if I’m on the right track or wrong track lol

Also to note I have it where you can select which group you need the item to be moved in, if needed.

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml:
You don’t have to download anything if it’s not needed those apps are hard to sell because data issue.

with monday SDK you can do anything almost client side or just you can use session tokens if you need to run on your server it’s valid for 5 mins

and if need long term access you oauth tokens
